interests: Strictly vegan diet, vegan or fully plant-based restaurants, cycling, vegan food markets, and eco-friendly activities

task_list.name: train_cologne_amsterdam
task_list.text: Search for train connections and prices from Cologne to Amsterdam for October 3rd and returning October 6th, 2026, for two people.

task_list.name: eco_certified_hotels_amsterdam

task_list.name: vegan_restaurants_amsterdam
task_list.text: Identify strictly vegan or fully plant-based restaurants in Amsterdam for lunch and dinner options.

task_list.text: Search for vegan food markets or eco-friendly markets occurring in Amsterdam between October 3rd and 6th, 2026.

task_list.text: Find bike rental shops and eco-friendly activities or tours in Amsterdam.

travelplan.title: Vegan Eco Amsterdam Trip for Two — Cologne to Amsterdam, Oct 3–6 2026

slots.name: ICE International: Cologne to Amsterdam
slots.description: Rail-first arrival from Köln Hauptbahnhof to Amsterdam Centraal. Exact 2026 timetable not yet released; direct ICE International usually takes about 2h40 and runs frequently. Book via DB/NS International once tickets open; seat reservations recommended.
slots.notes: Estimated cost for two one-way saver fares including seat reservations; adjust once live fares open.

slots.name: Transit to Conscious Hotel Westerpark
slots.description: From Amsterdam Centraal, take GVB Bus 21 toward Geuzenveld to Haarlemmerplein/Van Hallstraat, then walk to the hotel in Westerpark. Conservative transfer buffer included.
slots.notes: Routing API unavailable; web sources indicate roughly 10–15 minutes total.

slots.name: Lunch: Koffie ende Koeck
slots.description: 100% vegan and organic daytime café near Westerpark for pastries, sandwiches, coffee, and lunch after arrival.
slots.location: Koffie ende Koeck, Haarlemmerweg 175, 1051 LA Amsterdam
slots.notes: Open Wed–Sun 10:00–17:00 per sources; fully vegan/plant-powered.

slots.name: Check in: Conscious Hotel Westerpark
slots.description: Check into a Green Key Gold, 100% electric eco hotel in Westerpark. The hotel uses recycled materials, water-saving systems, organic food and beverage options, and cashless/self-service processes.
slots.location: Conscious Hotel Westerpark, Haarlemmerweg 10, 1014 BE Amsterdam
slots.notes: Estimated €180/night × 3 nights for two; live hotel tool alternatives ranged from €142–€250/night, while Conscious Westerpark was verified separately for sustainability credentials.

slots.name: Unconfirmed vegan market fallback: Westergas eco-food stroll
slots.description: Vegfest/Amsterdam Vegan Market is not currently confirmed for 3 Oct 2026, so treat Westergas as a flexible eco-food and culture stroll rather than a fixed festival booking. Check the official vegan market calendars again in early/mid-2026; if no event is confirmed, use this slot for Westerpark/Westergas cafés, galleries, and sustainable shops before dinner.
slots.notes: Replaces the earlier unverified Vegfest slot; no event ticket assumed.

slots.name: Dinner: Bonboon vegan fine dining
slots.description: Fully vegan fine-dining dinner in Jordaan with seasonal plant-based tasting menus. Reserve in advance, especially for Saturday.
slots.notes: Open Wed–Sun 18:00–23:00. Estimated two 5-course menus at about €62 each, drinks extra.

slots.name: Return to hotel from Jordaan
slots.description: Short tram, bus, bike, or walk back from Jordaan/Westermarkt area to Westerpark hotel.
slots.notes: Allow extra time if walking after dinner.

slots.name: Organic breakfast at hotel / Westerpark
slots.description: Start with the hotel’s organic breakfast or a simple vegan breakfast in Westerpark before the canal clean-up.
slots.location: Conscious Hotel Westerpark, Haarlemmerweg 10, 1014 BE Amsterdam
slots.notes: Confirm vegan items at booking/check-in; Conscious Hotels emphasize organic food and drink.

slots.name: Lunch: Hearth Amsterdam
slots.description: 100% plant-based lunch at Hearth in Amsterdam-Oost, known for creative vegan plates and natural wines.
slots.notes: Sunday hours include lunch service; reserve via official site/TheFork.

slots.name: Dinner: Vegan Junk Food Bar De Pijp
slots.description: Fully plant-based comfort-food dinner: burgers, loaded fries, and colorful vegan street-food plates at the De Pijp location.
slots.location: Vegan Junk Food Bar, Marie Heinekenplein 9-10, 1072 MH Amsterdam
slots.notes: Open Sunday until about 23:00; walk-ins common but peak times may have waits.

slots.name: Transit to plastic-fishing departure point
slots.description: Travel from Westerpark toward the central canal departure area for the plastic-fishing activity. Exact pier is confirmed on booking.
slots.location: Conscious Hotel Westerpark → central Amsterdam canal dock
slots.notes: Use tram/bus or bike depending on assigned departure pier.

slots.name: Eco activity: plastic fishing canal clean-up
slots.description: Hands-on canal clean-up by boat, collecting plastic from Amsterdam’s waterways. A sustainability-focused alternative to a standard canal cruise.
slots.location: Amsterdam canals; departure point confirmed after booking
slots.notes: Estimated €45.95 per person. Confirm whether individual bookings or minimum group sizes apply.

slots.name: Rent bikes for 24 hours
slots.description: Pick up standard city bikes for a 24-hour cycling window. Use hotel bike rental if available or MacBike as backup.
slots.location: Conscious Hotel Westerpark or MacBike Amsterdam
slots.notes: Estimated €15 per bike/day; MacBike sources showed about €12–€16 per 24 hours.

slots.name: Cycle Westerpark, Jordaan and canal belt
slots.description: Low-impact cycling loop from Westerpark through Jordaan and quieter canal-side streets, with café/photo stops and flexible pacing.
slots.notes: Keep to marked cycle lanes and use two locks when parking.

slots.name: Cycle or transit to De Pijp dinner
slots.description: Travel from Westerpark/city center to Vegan Junk Food Bar in De Pijp by bike or GVB transit.
slots.notes: Bike rental already budgeted; use transit if weather is poor.

slots.name: Return bikes / evening ride back
slots.description: Ride or take transit back to Westerpark after dinner; park bikes securely overnight if rental terms allow.
slots.notes: Avoid cycling if tired or weather is poor; Amsterdam transit is a good fallback.

slots.name: Bike ride to Albert Cuyp Market / De Pijp
slots.description: Use the 24-hour bike rental to ride from Westerpark toward De Pijp and Albert Cuyp Market, taking a conservative pace through cycle lanes.
slots.notes: Market vendors are usually fully set up by around 10:00–11:00.

slots.name: Lunch: Vegan Temple Bar
slots.description: Fully plant-based casual lunch near Leidseplein, with vegan burgers, bowls, nachos, and bar-style dishes.
slots.location: Vegan Temple Bar, Lange Leidsedwarsstraat 17, 1017 NE Amsterdam
slots.notes: Open daily from about 12:00 to 22:30; fully vegan/plant-based.

slots.name: Return bikes / rest at hotel
slots.description: Return the 24-hour rental bikes or park them securely if the rental is through the hotel, then take a short rest before the Noord sustainability ride.
slots.notes: If weather is poor, switch the afternoon to ferry + walking in Noord.

slots.name: Vegan food tour / Albert Cuyp Market
slots.description: Vegan-focused market walk in De Pijp/Albert Cuyp area with plant-based tastings and local food context. The outdoor market runs Monday–Saturday and has the strongest setup from late morning.
slots.notes: Approx. €21.50/person based on tour sources. Confirm the selected operator is strictly vegan before booking.

slots.name: Transit to Amsterdam Noord via Centraal ferry
slots.description: Leave the hotel/rest period earlier and allow a full hour to reach Amsterdam Noord: travel from Westerpark to Amsterdam Centraal, board the free IJ ferry, then continue to De Ceuvel by bike, bus, or walk. This fixes the previously unrealistic 15:30-to-16:00 transition.
slots.location: Conscious Hotel Westerpark → Amsterdam Centraal ferry → De Ceuvel
slots.notes: Route timing could not be live-verified because the Maps API key was unavailable; the itinerary now includes a conservative 60-minute buffer.

slots.name: Eco activity: De Ceuvel circular site and Amsterdam Noord
slots.description: Explore De Ceuvel, a circular urbanism site with houseboats on land, soil-cleaning plants, recycled materials, and Amsterdam-Noord sustainability projects. The later start allows realistic travel plus the IJ ferry crossing.
slots.location: De Ceuvel, Korte Papaverweg 2–6, 1032 KB Amsterdam
slots.notes: Keep this as a flexible self-guided visit; check café/site opening details closer to travel.

slots.name: Dinner: Trevi’s fully vegan Italian
slots.description: Unique Monday dinner at Trevi’s, a 100% vegan Italian restaurant in De Pijp serving plant-based pizza, handmade pasta, focaccia and vegan desserts. Monday evening hours are listed as 17:00–21:30; reserve ahead because seating is limited.
slots.notes: Chosen to replace the repeated Hearth dinner and broaden the vegan dining scene exploration.

days.label: Vegan brunch and return to Cologne

slots.name: Check out and store luggage at Amsterdam Centraal
slots.description: Check out by 11:00, then take bags to Amsterdam Centraal and place them in official NS luggage lockers in the East Wing or use nearby Lockerpoint if the station lockers are full. This makes the luggage plan explicit before lunch and the 15:00 train.
slots.location: Conscious Hotel Westerpark → Amsterdam Centraal luggage lockers
slots.notes: Search result indicated NS station lockers are generally 05:00–01:00, card payment only, with Lockerpoint as a 24/7 backup; allow extra wayfinding time due to station works.

slots.name: Vegan brunch/lunch: Vegan Temple Bar
slots.description: Final fully plant-based meal before departure, with reliable daily lunch hours and casual comfort-food options.
slots.location: Vegan Temple Bar, Lange Leidsedwarsstraat 17, 1017 NE Amsterdam
slots.notes: Chosen over Koffie ende Koeck because Tuesday closure was reported for Koffie ende Koeck.

slots.name: ICE International: Amsterdam to Cologne
slots.description: Rail return from Amsterdam Centraal to Köln Hauptbahnhof. Exact 2026 timetable not yet released; direct ICE International generally takes about 2h40.
slots.notes: Estimated two one-way saver fares with reservations; verify once booking opens.

slots.name: Flexible vegan dinner in Cologne after buffer
slots.description: Fully vegan post-trip dinner after a safer buffer from the 17:40 train arrival. The later start allows time for delays, platform exit, local transit/taxi, and a short reset before dinner.
slots.notes: Moved from 18:30 to 19:15 to avoid the previously tight Cologne arrival-to-dinner transition. Confirm opening/reservation closer to the date.

todos.title: Repair Day 4 luggage contradiction and make the departure timeline linear without overlaps
Replace Day 3 repeat Hearth dinner with a different fully vegan/plant-based restaurant
Check updated cost remains within €2,000 budget

It utilizes sustainable, eco-certified accommodation (Conscious Hotel Westerpark), prioritizes train travel via ICE International, and ensures every meal is at a strictly vegan or plant-based restaurant. The activities, including plastic fishing, cycling, and visiting De Ceuvel, align with the user's eco-friendly interests.
